strategies.summary: The presence of duplicated rows combined with near-perfect feature correlations may indicate that data redundancy inflates observed dependencies, potentially biasing downstream analysis and model training.
strategies.strategic_objective: Assess the impact of duplicated rows on feature correlation strength and dependency inflation to understand redundancy effects on dataset structure.

strategies.summary: The presence of perfect or near-perfect correlations among several feature pairs suggests strong linear dependencies that may introduce redundancy, potentially reducing the effective dimensionality of the dataset and influencing model learning dynamics.
strategies.strategic_objective: Investigate the stability and origin of strong feature pair dependencies to distinguish inherent protocol patterns from dataset artifacts.

strategies.summary: Backward packet length features exhibit substantial class-conditioned structural variation, with high variance ratios and skewed unique value counts between BENIGN and DDoS classes, indicating these features may carry discriminative but potentially unstable signals sensitive to class representation.
strategies.strategic_objective: Characterize the generalizability and stability of class-conditioned variation in backward packet length features under class imbalance and sampling biases.
